verify.value: Motor modules are removable without full teardown.
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

verify.value: Harnesses are routed through visible raceways.
verify.rule: [cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

verify.value: Access is represented by geometry: fastener pattern.
verify.rule: [access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Strain relief geometry and bend radius are present at cable exits.
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

verify.value: Connectors are reachable and have keying.
verify.rule: [connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

verify.value: Joint axis rings are visible.
verify.rule: [axis_datum_readability] good: J1-J6 axis rings, reducer/bearing dummies, encoder covers, and tool flange datum are visible. | bad: Arm links are generic cylinders with no joint service evidence.

verify.value: Cable spine is inspectable.
verify.rule: [cable_spine_serviceability] good: Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels. | bad: Cable routing is buried inside closed links.

verify.value: Joint cartridges are removable.
verify.rule: [joint_cartridge_replaceability] good: Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links. | bad: Reducers/bearings are fused into structural links.

verify.value: Bearings have seats, shoulders, and fit intent.
verify.rule: [bearing_shaft_mounting_sanity] good: Each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature, shaft locating geometry, and stated fit intent for inner/outer races. | bad: Bearing dummies float in links with no seat, shoulder, retaining ring groove, or axial location scheme.

verify.value: Harness has slack loops and strain relief.
verify.rule: [moving_joint_harness_workmanship] good: Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range. | bad: Cables cross joints taut, pass through pinch zones, or have no relief at link entry/exit.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.desc: Cable chafing risk at sharp edges.
risk.mit: Use edge radii and protective sleeving.

risk.desc: Bearing preload variation due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement torque control during assembly and periodic inspection.

messages.content: You are Blueprint XPU. Given a vehicle assembly brief, output a schema_v6 multi-subsystem blueprint as raw JSON (part_tree + coordinate-bearing geometry_ops + cad_brief + verify + risk per subsystem). No prose.

Design brief: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable
Seed: tiltrotor
Decompose into these subsystems and output the assembly blueprint JSON: Center Fuselage Spine, Main Wing (2-piece), Nacelle Assembly x 2, Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2, V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator), Landing Gear (Tri), Battery Bay (Slide-In), Payload Nose Bay, Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay

vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure
physics_paths: Load path: Nacelle -> Wing Spar Socket -> Fuselage Spine -> Landing Gear Mount
Flow path: Avionics bay ventilation -> Belly Camera Window
Thermal path: Motor heat -> Fuselage Spine -> Ventral Heat Sink
Motion path: Tiltrotor nacelles -> Tilt Axis -> Fuselage Spine

requirements: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

verify.value: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, and mating pads are visible and labeled.
verify.rule: good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled. | bad: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references.

verify.value: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances.
verify.rule: good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances. | bad: Moving/removable joints have no clearance or fit language.

verify.value: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums.
verify.rule: good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums. | bad: Subsystem is isolated and does not show assembly neighbors.

verify.value: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance.
verify.rule: good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance. | bad: All mating diameters are modeled line-to-line with identical nominal dimensions and no fit class or allowance.

verify.value: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters).
verify.rule: good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters). | bad: Hardware features use arbitrary dimensions that match no purchasable fastener, bearing, or pin.

verify.value: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible.
verify.rule: good: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible. | bad: Nacelle is a generic motor pod without tilt-axis evidence.

verify.value: Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable.
verify.rule: good: Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable. | bad: Wing/fuselage is a blended block with no interface features.

verify.value: Output remains educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details.
verify.rule: good: Output remains educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details. | bad: Design claims BVLOS capability, flight readiness, or actionable aircraft tuning.

verify.value: Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers.
verify.rule: good: Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers. | bad: Nacelle attaches by an undefined blend; motor and servo are entombed with no removal path or load fitting.

verify.value: cad_brief.mass_est_g is within acceptable limits.
verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.desc: Potential for vibration fatigue at wing spar socket attachment points.
risk.mit: Use of robust fastening system with appropriate preload and torque values.

risk.desc: Risk of interference between avionics components and landing gear mechanism.
risk.mit: Careful layout and routing of cables and components, with dedicated service access panels.

physics_paths: load_path: ring transmits stage separation loads to vehicle frame via bolted connections
inspection_access: visual access through inspection window for checklist verification
checklist_callout: physical markers correspond to checklist items

vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Equipment Bay and Motor Mount
physics_paths: Load path from wing spar to motor pylon
Cooling airflow intake to pusher prop
Harness routing for avionics in equipment bay

requirements: House avionics and equipment in a sealed bay.
Provide structural support for the wing spar and tail boom.
Mount the pusher propeller motor with a cooling path.
Allow access to internal components for maintenance.

verify.value: Parts are integrated only when service access, inspection, and interface clarity remain visible.
verify.rule: good: Parts are integrated only when service access, inspection, and interface clarity remain visible. | bad: Monolithic body hides removable modules, datums, or inspection paths.

verify.value: Fastener patterns, pin joints, slots, bosses, and mating clearances are explicitly represented.
verify.rule: good: Fastener patterns, pin joints, slots, bosses, and mating clearances are explicitly represented. | bad: Joints are described in prose but not represented as holes, bosses, pins, slots, or datums.

verify.value: A reviewer can see what to inspect and why without opening raw JSON.
verify.rule: good: A reviewer can see what to inspect and why without opening raw JSON. | bad: Design has no callouts, access windows, labels, datum pads, or verification focus.

verify.value: Critical joints state locking method (locknut, insert, washer, adhesive note) and torque/preload intent; bolt patterns match load direction.
verify.rule: good: Critical joints state locking method (locknut, insert, washer, adhesive note) and torque/preload intent; bolt patterns match load direction. | bad: Fasteners appear as bare holes with no locking, washer, engagement-length, or preload language anywhere.

verify.value: Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole.
verify.rule: good: Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole. | bad: Screws thread directly into thin printed walls or blind features with no boss, insert, or depth note.

verify.value: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review.
verify.rule: good: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review. | bad: Manufacturing process is absent or contradicts geometry.

verify.value: Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops.
verify.rule: good: Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops. | bad: Verification is generic pass/warn text not tied to features.

verify.value: Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming.
verify.rule: good: Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming. | bad: Risks are missing or only mention vague quality concerns.

verify.value: Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface.
verify.rule: good: Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface. | bad: Requirements and verification lists exist independently with no traceable pairing.

verify.value: Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid), like a miniature interface control entry.
verify.rule: good: Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid), like a miniature interface control entry. | bad: Subsystems reference each other by name only, with no statement of what crosses the interface.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.desc: Bolt preload on motor mount may loosen due to vibration.
risk.mit: Use locking nuts and torque to specification.

risk.desc: Cooling airflow path could be blocked by equipment bay contents.
risk.mit: Design baffle or screen to protect intake.

risk.desc: Access hatch hinges may fatigue over time.
risk.mit: Use robust hinge design and material selection.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Equipment Bay and Motor Mount
physics_paths: Load path from wing spar to motor pylon
Cooling airflow intake to pusher prop
Harness routing for avionics in equipment bay

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
physics_paths: Lift loads from wing skin to spar socket
Landing impact through spar to fuselage frame
Payload weight transfer via carry-through structure

requirements: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key

verify.value: Spar socket faces visible and labeled
verify.rule: Good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled. | Bad: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references.

verify.value: Slip fit clearances not explicitly stated for spar halves.
verify.rule: Good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances. | Bad: Moving/removable joints have no clearance or fit language.

verify.value: 3 adjacent interfaces: Wing Skin (Left), Wing Skin (Right), Fuselage Frame
verify.rule: Good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums. | Bad: Subsystem is isolated and does not show assembly neighbors.

verify.value: Bolted connections use clearance fit, consistent with standard practice.
verify.rule: Good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance. | Bad: All mating diameters are modeled line-to-line with identical nominal dimensions and no fit class or allowance.

verify.value: M3 bolts used for spar-to-skin connections.
verify.rule: Good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters). | Bad: Hardware features use arbitrary dimensions that match no purchasable fastener, bearing, or pin.

verify.value: Spar socket faces remain accessible.
verify.rule: Good: Spar socket, anti-rotation keys, and center datum remain visible and inspectable. | Bad: Wing/fuselage integration hides spar alignment evidence.

verify.value: Not applicable to wing spar subsystem.
verify.rule: Good: Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module. | Bad: Design drifts into weaponized or operational payload details.

verify.value: Not applicable to wing spar subsystem.
verify.rule: Good: Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal. | Bad: Battery is buried or coupled to payload in a way that blocks CG adjustment.

verify.value: Loads transfer through spar to carry-through to fuselage frame.
verify.rule: Good: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer. | Bad: Structural members end at skins or bays with no fitting, frame, or carry-through; load path breaks at the wing root.

risk.mit: Improved fixturing and alignment tools

verify.value: Parts are integrated only when service access, inspection, and interface clarity remain visible.
Fastener patterns, pin joints, slots, bosses, and mating clearances are explicitly represented.
A reviewer can see what to inspect and why without opening raw JSON. However, some callouts or datum pads could be added for clarity.
Critical joints state locking method (locknut) and torque/preload intent; bolt patterns match load direction.
Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole.
Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review.
Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops.
Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface.
Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid), like a miniature interface control entry.

verify.value: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload modules remain removable without full teardown.
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

verify.value: Harnesses routed through visible raceways.
verify.rule: [cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

verify.value: Service hatch provides physical access.
verify.rule: [access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Harness exits have strain relief and respect bend radius.
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

verify.value: Connectors reachable with finger/tool clearance and keying.
verify.rule: [connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

verify.value: Primary datum pads are visible and labeled.
verify.rule: [datum_visibility] good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled. | bad: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references.

verify.value: Critical slip, snap, pin interfaces state plausible clearances.
verify.rule: [clearance_named] good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances. | bad: Moving/removable joints have no clearance or fit language.

verify.value: Subsystem has 3 adjacent interfaces.
verify.rule: [interface_count] good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums. | bad: Subsystem is isolated and does not show assembly neighbors.

verify.value: Fits are named with standard intent (clearance, transition).
verify.rule: [standard_fit_vocabulary] good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance. | bad: All mating diameters are modeled line-to-line with identical nominal dimensions and no fit class or allowance.

verify.value: Holes match real catalog hardware sizes.
verify.rule: [standard_hardware_dimensioning] good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters). | bad: Hardware features use arbitrary dimensions that match no purchasable fastener, bearing, or pin.

verify.value: Material and process are explicit.
verify.rule: [wall_process_readiness] good: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review. | bad: Manufacturing process is absent or contradicts geometry.

verify.value: Estimated mass is 250g, target was 200g.
verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.desc: Connector mis-mating due to insufficient keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Implement robust connector keying and clear labeling.

risk.desc: Cable chafing or damage at sharp edges or tight bends.
risk.mit: Ensure strain relief features are present and routing respects bend radius.

vehicle.label: Force-Feedback Haptic Glove Pair
vehicle.desc: Wearable force-feedback exo-glove pair for VR grasp resistance, serviceable actuator cartridges, linkages, wrist band, and dorsal control module

parts.label: Dorsal Control Module x 2
parts.spec: Snap-in dorsal electronics and battery module, tool-less lid, wireless board bay, service label and cable exits

parts.label: Finger Exo Linkage Cartridge x 10
parts.spec: Two-segment exoskeleton finger linkage cartridge, single-pin removable, hinge datum pads, thimble slip interface

parts.label: Flexion-Resistance Actuator x 10
parts.spec: Plug-in tendon brake/clutch actuator module, knuckle row mount, cable pulley, connector pocket, service access

parts.label: Adjustable Wrist Band x 2
parts.spec: Quick-release wrist band, cable raceway, soft-contact interface, buckle, dorsal module anchor

verify.value: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels.

verify.value: Battery module is removable via quick-swap door
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

verify.value: Cable routing channels are visible and accessible
verify.rule: [cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

verify.value: Quick-swap door provides physical access to battery compartment
verify.rule: [access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Strain relief features are incorporated at cable exits
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

verify.value: Power connector is accessible and keyed for proper mating
verify.rule: [connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

risk.desc: BMS access window may be susceptible to dust ingress
risk.mit: Implement a gasket seal around the window
Use threadlocker on critical fasteners

requirements: Knuckle-crossing wiring loom with service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, flex-rated bend radius, power vs signal channel separation

verify.value: Battery, sensor, actuator cartridges are removable
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

verify.value: Cables routed through visible channels
verify.rule: [cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

verify.value: Access hatches provided for connectors
verify.rule: [access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Strain relief boots at flex points
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

verify.value: Connectors accessible with clearance
verify.rule: [connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

verify.value: Cartridges removable via hinge
verify.rule: [finger_cartridge_service] good: Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access. | bad: Finger linkages are glued, buried, or represented only as a glove shell.

verify.value: Actuators plug in and remain visible
verify.rule: [actuator_module_access] good: Flexion-resistance actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row. | bad: Actuators are bonded or hidden without connector/service geometry.

verify.value: Rigid mock assembly preserves soft contact cues
verify.rule: [wearable_comfort_boundary] good: Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance. | bad: Rigid shell ignores don/doff, comfort, and finger clearance.

verify.value: Service loops at flex points
verify.rule: [flex_cycle_harness_durability] good: Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles. | bad: Wires run taut across knuckles or terminate at rigid mounts with no relief, inviting fatigue failure.

verify.value: Power and signal lines separated
verify.rule: [power_signal_separation] good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry. | bad: Power and signal wires share one tight bundle along the whole route with no separation or shielding note.

verify.value: Grounding scheme documented
verify.rule: [return_path_and_grounding_intent] good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals. | bad: Grounding is unmentioned; returns are implied through structure with no bond feature or note.

verify.value: PCBs mounted on standoffs
verify.rule: [board_mounting_and_clearance] good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns. | bad: Boards are floated or glued in cavities with no standoffs, clearance, or connector orientation logic.

verify.value: Connector mates specified
verify.rule: [connection_type_named] good: Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type (bolted, threaded_insert, snap_fit, press_fit, dovetail_slide, clevis_pin, revolute_bearing, band_clamp, adhesive_lap, o_ring, connector_mate, harness_passthrough) with its DOF and a numeric clearance. | bad: Interfaces are labeled only 'mount' or 'attach' with no connection type, DOF, or clearance.

verify.value: Assembly sequence documented
verify.rule: [assembly_sequence_feasible] good: Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable; no installed part blocks access to a later one; last-installed and captive parts are identified. | bad: Assembly order is unstated or impossible — a part traps another, or a fastener ends up behind a wall with no access.

verify.value: Datum chain established
verify.rule: [datum_chain_continuity] good: One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme, so tolerance does not accumulate uncontrolled across the stack. | bad: Parts float with no shared datum, or each part references a different feature so stack-up grows part-to-part.

verify.value: Load path traced to ground
verify.rule: [load_path_closure] good: Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts and high-load joints use load-appropriate connection types. | bad: One or more parts carry load with no continuous path to ground, or a high-load joint uses a low-load connection (e.g. snap-fit holding a structural member).

verify.value: Design moves in the right direction
verify.rule: [structural_development_direction] good: Compared to the previous version the design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints. | bad: Iteration adds interfaces or loose parts without justification, or consolidates in a way that buries service access.

verify.value: Interfaces minimized
verify.rule: [interface_minimization] good: The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture; each remaining interface is justified by service, motion, manufacture split, or load transfer. | bad: Many redundant or unexplained interfaces, or arbitrary part splits that add joints without a service/manufacture reason.

verify.value: Mass estimate within budget
verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.mit: Use strain relief boots with appropriate bend radius
Ensure connector alignment features are precise and robust
Secure cartridges with reliable locking mechanism
